articles.title: Betting on Yourself: Three Congressional Candidates Fined for Wagering on Their Own Elections
articles.summary: Imagine being so sure you'd win an election that you bet money on yourself. That's exactly what three congressional candidates did on a prediction market called Kalshi. But Kalshi has a rule: candidates can't bet on themselves. When the company found out, it fined and suspended Mark Moran, Ezekiel Enriquez, and Matt Klein for five years. Moran, running for Senate in Virginia, bet $100 and bragged online, so he got the biggest fine: over $6,200. Klein and Enriquez each bet under $100 and paid smaller fines. Some politicians, like Rep. Mike Levin, say the penalties are too weak — just 'a parking ticket.' The fines came from the company, not the government agency that oversees prediction markets. Klein admitted his…

articles.title: Mind Over Washington: Mentalist Oz Pearlman to Stun Trump and Reporters at White House Dinner
articles.summary: Imagine being able to read someone's mind — even the president's! That's what mentalist Oz Pearlman will try to do at the White House Correspondents' Dinner this Saturday. Instead of a comedian, the 43-year-old performer will take the stage to wow President Donald Trump and hundreds of reporters. Pearlman is famous for guessing secret words and PIN numbers, a trick called mentalism. He got his big break on America's Got Talent in 2015. Pearlman has been planning one amazing moment for 10 years, and he hopes to bring everyone together. Even though experts say it's not real mind-reading, the show promises to be unforgettable. Will he actually read Trump's mind? We'll find out Saturday night!

articles.title: Iranian Leaders Unite to Reject Trump's Claims of a Rift: 'We Are One Soul, One Nation'
articles.summary: Imagine a country where everyone is saying the exact same thing at the exact same time — that's what happened in Iran this week. President Trump claimed Iran's leaders were fighting each other, but they proved him wrong. Iran's president, foreign minister, and parliament speaker all posted the same message on X: 'We are one soul, one nation.' This show of unity comes after a huge event — Iran's former Supreme Leader was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes on February 28. Now, the U.S. has blocked Iranian ports, and Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for oil. Oil prices are going up as a result. Trump warned he would 'shoot and kill' anyone laying mines in the strait.…
